item.title: Research
item.description: Research Interests My main field of interest is software security, including static analysis and instrumentation, memory safety, and secure software design. These interests manifest mainly in work with the LLVM compiler framework and the Rust programming language’s compiler. I’m driven to create methods to securely gradually transition from unsafe languages like C/C++ to memory-safe languages, especially Rust. Since the beginning of my PhD, I am very attracted to Rust because its ownership-based type system allows writing memory-safe programs even for low-level targets since no garbage collection is needed.
